✨Client Success Stories✨ (Check link in bio for FREE resources)

💡Do YOU want to be featured? Share your success story using #WonsultingWinners ! We'd love to shout you all out ❤️

MEET: Alejandra Huamán 📌
"Wonsulting first helped me with my resume. I can see an incredible improvement from my previous version to the one edited by Wonsulting, specially results. But most importantly asking me the right questions to fill those gaps with impacting results and actions words. The interview prep helped me understand the importance of curiosity and non-verbal communication, the tone and energy you transmit while articulating the interview questions. I also found resources that helped me structure my answers from IG and TikTok and Wonsulting's deck sample was really helpful (Facebook Proposal). And those examples from Wonsulting in the YouTube channel. There I could see how using the right words within 1-2 min can make an impact in your career. The interview process from my current job, it felt like a conversation 60%. I felt optimistic from the beginning, it was all or nothing. My Canadian visa was about to expire, and got the job last weekday possible, Friday about 7pm. I was in the job search from Jan 21, and focused 100% from May 21, 2 exciting opportunities from June (#1 multiple rounds, at least 4, #2 3 steps around 2 weeks) got the job early September from the second opportunity."

✨ If YOU need help on your job search, check out our services from the link in our bio to help land you a job in 2022!
.

We’re sharing our 2024 strategy with you all.  We’ve seen the headlines. And while we can’t control the macroeconomic environments, we can control how we structure your job search.  This is what you should expect:

💡Q1: You’ll be able to track all your applications, interviews, and offers in 1 place, automatically. Yes it’ll be free. 

💡Q2: We’ll tell you where you’re stuck in the job search process and provide recommendations.  
Give us a few months and we’re going to simplify your job search like you’ve never seen before. 

So excited to have you along on this journey!! Comment below if you’re as pumped as we are!

Ford Motor Company just rehired 300 veteran engineers. Turns out, AI alone couldn't fix their quality problems, costing them billions. This is a big win for human expertise. Your years of experience are still invaluable, even with all the new tech out there.

Source: Business Insider (https://www.businessinsider.com/ford-ai-hiring-veteran-engineers-2026-6)

Amazon is hiring 11,000 interns and new grads by 2026. AWS CEO Matt Garman says forget just coding skills. They want adaptability, curiosity, and fast learners because AI is handling routine tasks. For job seekers, this means highlighting your growth mindset and learning ability is key.

Source: Timesofindia (https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/technology/tech-news/as-amazon-is-hiring-11000-interns-three-qualities-that-amazon-web-services-ceo-matt-garman-says-the-company-is-looking-for/articleshow/132158529.cms)

Workday, a major HR software provider, is facing a class-action lawsuit over alleged AI bias. The suit claims their job screening tools discriminate against Black applicants, those over 40, and people with disabilities. A federal judge just ruled the case can proceed, potentially impacting millions of job seekers. This is why you can't just rely on online applications alone.

Source: Wftv (wftv.com/news/trending/workday-faces-lawsuit-over-ai-bias-job-application-screenings/…)

New data from Bandana shows NYC job seekers are prioritizing stability and benefits. Public sector, civil service, and healthcare jobs are booming. Think NYU Langone Health and Mount Sinai. If you're looking for a role, these industries are where the action is.

Source: EyeWitness News (https://abc7ny.com/post/top-10-most-popular-jobs-nyc-spring-according-job-search-platform-bandana-7-side/18856372/)

The economy added 172,000 jobs in May, making it three straight months of gains. But for millions of job seekers, it's a completely different story. Recent grads and long-term unemployed are still struggling to find work, often facing a skills mismatch and low hiring rates. This shows it's truly a tale of two realities out there.

Source: Business Insider (https://www.businessinsider.com/layoffs-job-market-unemployed-americans-2026-6)

U.S. job openings surged to 7.6 million in April, hitting a two-year high. That sounds great, but hiring actually fell sharply, especially in professional and business services. Companies are posting more roles, but they aren't rushing to fill them. If you're job searching, know that the process might take longer. 

Source: CNBC (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/02/job-openings-april-2026.html)

Ever heard of 'doomjobbing'? It's a new term for compulsively scrolling job boards and mass-applying, and experts say it’s actually hurting your job search. This reactive behavior, driven by job market stress and AI uncertainty, increases anxiety and leads to burnout for job seekers. Wonsulting knows it feels productive, but generic applications often get ignored. Focus on targeted applications and strategic networking instead.

Source: CNBC (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/19/doomjobbing-can-hurt-your-job-search-why-it-happens-and-how-to-avoid-it.html)

Dave Lewis got laid off from Amazon and Microsoft, twice in three years. He then focused his job search on where tech is going next: AI. This strategic pivot landed him a new role at an AI startup. That's a strong playbook for today's market.

Source: Business Insider (https://www.businessinsider.com/laid-off-amazon-microsoft-millennial-tech-job-market-artificial-intelligence-2026-6)

Anthropic just launched their Claude Corps National AI Fellowship. They're investing $150 million to train 1,000 early-career professionals in Claude AI, paying $85,000 plus benefits for a year. This is a big deal for anyone looking to break into the AI space with a full-time, paid role. Keep an eye on programs like this to upskill and land new opportunities.

Source: Anthropic (https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-corps)
